2603.19022.title: Behavioral Fingerprints for LLM Endpoint Stability and Identity
2603.19022.paper_summary: The consistency of AI-native applications depends on the behavioral consistency of the model endpoints that power them. Traditional reliability metrics such as uptime, latency and throughput do not capture behavioral change, and an endpoint can remain "healthy" while its effective model identity changes due to updates to weights, tokenizers, quantization, inference engines, kernels, caching, routing, or hardware. We introduce Stability Monitor, a black-box stability monitoring system that periodically fingerprints an endpoint by sampling outputs from a fixed prompt set and comparing the resulting output distributions over time. Fingerprints are compared using a summed energy distance statistic across prompts, with permutation-test p-values as evidence of distribution shift aggregated sequentially to detect change events and define stability periods. In controlled validation, Stability Monitor detects changes to model family, version, inference stack, quantization, and behavioral parameters. In real-world monitoring of the same model hosted by multiple providers, we observe substantial provider-to-provider and within-provider stability differences.
2603.19022.paper_summary_zh: AI 原生應用的穩定性取決於驅動它們的模型端點的行為一致性。傳統的可靠性指標，如正常運行時間、延遲和吞吐量，無法捕捉行為變化，而一個端點在其有效模型身份因權重、分詞器、量化、推理引擎、內核、緩存、路由或硬體的更新而改變時，仍然可以保持“健康”。我們引入了穩定性監控器（Stability Monitor），這是一種黑箱穩定性監控系統，通過從固定的提示集取樣輸出並隨時間比較結果輸出分佈，定期對端點進行指紋識別。指紋通過在提示上使用總能量距離統計進行比較，並以置換檢驗的 p 值作為分佈變化的證據，這些證據按順序聚合以檢測變化事件並定義穩定期。在受控驗證中，穩定性監控器能夠檢測到模型系列、版本、推理堆棧、量化和行為參數的變化。在對由多個提供者托管的相同模型進行的實際監控中，我們觀察到提供者之間以及提供者內部的穩定性差異顯著。
2603.19022.author: Jonah Leshin et.al.
2603.19022.authors: Jonah Leshin, Manish Shah, Ian Timmis, Daniel Kang
